title: Crumbly Fermented Uruguayan Beef Empanadas
description: A traditional Uruguayan dish featuring fermented beef filling wrapped in a crumbly pastry, perfect for a cozy meal. This recipe combines the flavors of beef, onions, and Mediterranean spices.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine flour, yeast, and salt. Gradually add water and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rest for 1 hour.
In a separate bowl, combine beef, onion, garlic, paprika, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the beef mixture and cook until the beef is browned, about 5 minutes.
Let the beef mixture cool to room temperature.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces. Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each circle. Fold the dough in half and press the edges to seal.
Let the empanadas rest for 30 minutes to allow the yeast to ferment.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C). Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
